narrative: OMX30 opens into a genuinely bullish setup: global equities printed strong gains (NASDAQ +206bps, DAX +88bps) and correlation signal at +67bps provides tailwind. The index sits +8.4% above its 200-day moving average in a bull regime with MACD above signal and ADX 35.2 confirming trend strength. However, technical overbought conditions (RSI 70.31, Stochastic K=96) and proximity to 52W highs (-1.0%) warrant tactical caution. Copper's +235bps surge and gold's +28bps directly benefit Boliden; oil +118bps pressures input-cost sensitive industrials. Yields down 46bps (US 10Y to 4.372%) is a mild headwind for banks but removes DCF compression pressure from Tech.
morningBrief: OMX30 should open bid on overnight NASDAQ strength (+2.06%) and broad European rally; expect opening within +0.4% to +0.7% range. Key watch: hold above 3123 (yesterday's close) with initial resistance at 3180 (50-MA). Copper's monster +235bps move makes Boliden the stock to own in Morning Trading—direct leverage to metals upside. Industrials (Atlas Copco, Volvo B, Sandvik) mixed: USD/SEK stable (9.735) offers exporter support, but oil +118bps adds cost headwinds; play the sector selectively. Banks neutral to slightly positive (SEB A, Handelsbanken A) as yield compression is modest. CRITICAL: RSI 70.31 and Stochastic K=96 signal mean reversion risk—avoid chasing rips into resistance; manage position size and target tactical exits near 3165-3175. Watch US Jobless Claims (14:30 CET) for session inflection.
drivers: Strong global equity backdrop (DAX +0.88%, NASDAQ +2.06%, S&P +1.18%) with 0.674% weighted correlation signal
OMX30 in established bull market: +8.4% above 200-MA, MACD above signal, ADX 35.2 (strong trend)
Commodity strength (copper +2.35%, oil +1.18%) supporting industrials and mining sectors
RSI 70.31 overbought and Stochastic K=96 flagging mean reversion risk; -1.0% from 52W high creates caution

sectorImpacts.reason: Copper +2.35% and gold +0.28% directly expand revenue base; copper rally particularly potent for Boliden's mix.
USD/SEK stable at 9.735 supports exporter margins; however oil +1.18% raises input costs. Net effect slightly negative for cyclical exposure.
US 10Y yield down 46bps to 4.372% narrows NIM expectations marginally; offset by lower refinancing costs.
NASDAQ +2.06% overnight provides positive sentiment; MACD above signal removes DCF headwinds from yield compression.
Yield down 46bps is modest refinancing relief; lower cost of capital a minor tailwind.
Oil +1.18% pressures consumer spending; EUR/SEK +0.01% is immaterial but H&M sourcing costs edge higher.

watchList.reason: Copper +2.35% and gold +0.28% provide direct revenue lift; mining commodity complex in risk-on mode.
NASDAQ +2.06% overnight and removal of DCF headwinds from falling yields; sentiment boost for Tech.
Exporter with USD/SEK support (9.735 stable); balanced against oil +118bps input cost headwind. Play breakout above 3180 (50-MA).
Dual exposure: exporter benefit from stable USD/SEK offset by oil cost pressure. Best to wait for clear direction post-jobless claims.
Oil +1.18% pressures consumer discretionary spending and sourcing costs; EUR/SEK edging higher.

econCalendarAlert: No major Swedish economic releases today. US Jobless Claims (initial & continuing) at 14:30 CET—key session inflection risk. Monitor for yield reaction if claims surprise.
topRisk: Mean reversion in overbought OMX30 (RSI 70.31, Stochastic K=96) into resistance 3180–3200. Profit-taking likely into US data release. Secondary risk: escalation in geopolitical tensions (Putin/Ukraine narrative in FT) could trigger risk-off and spike VIX (currently 17.59, benign).
technicalSummary: MACD above signal with histogram +3.754 (strong momentum)
ADX 35.2 with bullish DI bias (trend strength intact)
RSI 70.31 and Stochastic K=96/D=83.3 both overbought—mean reversion risk
52W range at 96%, only -1.0% from high (limited upside room without breakout)
